category_title: Preschoolers who use unfriendly or bossy language during peer play
student_description: Nia is a highly social preschooler who loves organizing play but struggles with impulsivity, often using a rude or bossy tone that hurts her peers' feelings. She uniquely requires concrete, in-the-moment scripts to rephrase her negative comments into friendly ones, as well as proactive visual priming before play sessions to model respectful communication.

criteria.rationale: Nia responds well to having clear, visual expectations set immediately before she enters a highly stimulating social environment.
criteria.check: The long-term plan includes using video clips or similar visual models to prime Nia for successful interactions before free play sessions begin.

criteria.rationale: To internalize positive social skills, Nia needs dedicated time outside of heated moments to break down the mechanics of being a good friend.
criteria.check: The long-term plan incorporates dedicated discussions about how friends respectfully talk, compliment, and ask to help one another.
